Obviously not all women wore these hairstyles. I'm mostly highlighting the beauty ideal. During the Victorian/Edwardian eras, long hair was sought after but it was almost always styled up or coiled into a bun. In the 20s, popular actresses wore the bob cut which was a dramatic change from earlier fashion.

A fragile porcelain cup holding black and bitter coffee, much needed after a night of unsteady dreams. Ink flowing from a trusted fountain pen onto dry paper, each stroke deliberate, each word intended. 18th-century books whispering in worn bindings, crimson wax poured on forgotten letters. I’m nothing in my soul if not sentimental and melancholic. 🕰️☕️🗝️
